description: CMS admin UI for editing the 4 landing pages (Startsida, Om oss, Tjänster, Kontakt) — tab-based editor with fixed form fields per page, save, and publish toggle

userStories.title: Ny sidebar-länk Webbplats
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a 'Webbplats' link in the sidebar so that I can navigate to the CMS editor.
acceptanceCriteria: New sidebar group 'Webbplats' with a Globe icon added below the main nav group
Links to /webbplats route
Active state highlights correctly when on /webbplats
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Follow existing sidebar patterns in the admin app. The route does not need to exist yet — just the sidebar link.

userStories.title: CMS-sida med flikar för de 4 sidorna
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see tabs for Startsida, Om oss, Tjänster, and Kontakt so that I can switch between pages to edit.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /webbplats created at apps/admin/src/routes/_authenticated/webbplats.tsx
4 tabs rendered: Startsida, Om oss, Tjänster, Kontakt
Each tab loads the corresponding page data from api.cms.getPage using the slug (startsida, om-oss, tjanster, kontakt)
When no CMS data exists yet, the form fields are empty (not pre-filled with fallback content)
Active tab persists via URL search param (e.g. ?sida=om-oss)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use shadcn/ui Tabs component. Use TanStack Router search params for active tab. Each tab should render a placeholder for now — the actual form content comes in US-003 through US-006.

userStories.title: Formulär för Startsida
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a form with fields matching the Startsida sections so that I can edit its content.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section: text input for title, textarea for subtitle, text input for CTA text, text input for CTA href

Features section: text input for section title, textarea for section subtitle, plus 6 fixed item groups each with text input for title, textarea for description, icon selector dropdown (ClipboardCheck, Wrench, BarChart3, Shield, Building2, Phone)
Stats section: 4 fixed item groups each with text input for value (mapped to item title), text input for label (mapped to item description)
CTA section: text input for title, textarea for subtitle, text input for CTA text, text input for CTA href
Each section is a visually distinct Card with a section heading
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Section types in the CMS: hero (order 0), features (order 1), stats (order 2), cta (order 3). Stats maps value→item.title, label→item.description. Use shadcn Card, Input, Textarea, Label, Select components.

userStories.title: Formulär för Om oss
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a form with fields matching the Om oss sections so that I can edit its content.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section: text input for title, textarea for subtitle
Mission section: text input for title, textarea for content
Values section: text input for section title, plus 4 fixed item groups each with text input for title, textarea for description, icon selector (Target, Eye, Users, Award)
Story section: text input for title, textarea for content (paragraphs separated by blank lines \n\n)
Each section is a visually distinct Card with a section heading
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Section types: hero (order 0), mission (order 1), values (order 2), story (order 3). Story content uses the 'content' field with \n\n paragraph separators.

userStories.title: Formulär för Tjänster
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a form with fields matching the Tjänster sections so that I can edit its content.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section: text input for title, textarea for subtitle
Services section: 6 fixed service groups each with text input for title, textarea for description, icon selector (ClipboardCheck, Wrench, BarChart3, Shield, Building2, Phone)
Each section is a visually distinct Card with a section heading
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Section types: hero (order 0), services (order 1). The hardcoded CTA at the bottom of the Tjänster page is NOT part of the CMS — do not add fields for it. Per-service feature bullet lists are also excluded from CMS.

userStories.title: Formulär för Kontakt
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a form with fields matching the Kontakt sections so that I can edit its content.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section: text input for title, textarea for subtitle

Contact section: text input for section title, textarea for section subtitle, plus 4 fixed item groups each with text input for title, text input for description, icon selector (Mail, Phone, MapPin, Clock)
Form section: text input for title, textarea for subtitle
Each section is a visually distinct Card with a section heading
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Section types: hero (order 0), contact (order 1), form (order 2). Contact items use text input (not textarea) for description since they are short values like email/phone.

userStories.title: Spara innehåll
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to save my edits so that the landing page reflects the new content.
acceptanceCriteria: Spara button at the bottom of the form
If no page exists in the database for this slug, calls api.cms.createPage with the form data
If a page already exists, calls api.cms.updatePage with the changed data
Form data is converted to the correct sections array format (each section has id, type, order, and relevant fields matching sectionValidator in convex/cms.ts)
Shows a success toast after successful save
Shows an error toast if save fails
Button shows loading state while saving
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use useMutation from convex/react. The sections array must match sectionValidator: { id, type, title?, subtitle?, content?, items?, cta?, imageUrl?, order }. Determine create vs update by checking if the useQuery result for the current slug returned a document (has _id).

userStories.title: Publicera/avpublicera toggle
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to toggle whether a page is published so that I can prepare content before making it live.
acceptanceCriteria: Switch/toggle labeled 'Publicerad' in the form header area next to the page title
Toggle state is saved together with the page content when clicking Spara
Visual indicator showing current publish state (green badge 'Publicerad' / gray badge 'Utkast')
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The published field is a boolean on the pages table. Include it in the createPage/updatePage payload alongside sections. Use shadcn Switch component.
